feed.title: SAP Community - SAP BTP Security
feed.subtitle: SAP BTP Security Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: The JWT token only contains the openid scope, but none of the custom application scopes
entry.content:
Hi,

I am building CAP application and try to add custom roles and rolecollections through xs-security.json. Deploying in CF. 

When i deploy my xs-auth service, i can see roles and rolecollections being created, i can see them in BTP cockpit Rolecollections, Roles . But what is missing is, i cant see roles under rolecollections. I tried below -

1) Created just roles through xs-security.json and created rolecollections manually in BTP. when try to browse and attach roles to rolecollection, i cant find my roles, templates or application identifier.

2) Created both roles and rolecollections through xs-security.json - i cant see roles inside rolecollections.

3) Removed template $XSAPPNAME from "role-template-references" as per recommendations, but i cant deploy, it gives error.

4) I tried delete and recreated whole auth service with new template, application name, identifier... no luck.

I am getting authenticated, but authorization is failing. My application require annotations and actions to be controlled (who can press button and perform action, this require custom roles). 

Attached template i am using. I am sure you might have similar situations before. Please let me know what is the best way to resolve this issue. As Always, thanks for your help.

Below is my app trace log.

template used as below:

{
"xsappname": "my-app-reprocess-v2",
"tenant-mode": "dedicated",
"description": "Message Reprocessing Application - Security Configuration",
"scopes": [
{
"name": "$XSAPPNAME.ReprocessViewer",
"description": "View messages, content, and statistics (read-only access)"
},
{
"name": "$XSAPPNAME.ReprocessAdministrator",
"description": "Full administrative access including reprocess operations and CRUD"
}
],
"attributes": [
{
"name": "email",
"description": "User email address",
"valueType": "string"
}
],
"role-templates": [
{
"name": "ReprocessViewer",
"description": "Read-Only Access - View messages and statistics",
"scope-references": [
"$XSAPPNAME.ReprocessViewer"
],
"attribute-references": [
"email"
]
},
{
"name": "ReprocessAdministrator",
"description": "Full Administrative Access - CRUD operations and reprocess actions",
"scope-references": [
"$XSAPPNAME.ReprocessAdministrator",
"$XSAPPNAME.ReprocessViewer"
],
"attribute-references": [
"email"
]
}
],
"role-collections": [
{
"name": "MessageReprocessViewersRC",
"description": "Message Reprocess Viewers - Read-only access",
"role-template-references": [
"$XSAPPNAME.ReprocessViewer"
]
},
{
"name": "MessageReprocessAdministratorsRC",
"description": "Message Reprocess Administrators - Full access",
"role-template-references": [
"$XSAPPNAME.ReprocessAdministrator"
]
}
],
"oauth2-configuration": {
"credential-types": ["binding-secret", "x509"],
"redirect-uris": [
"https://*.cfapps.example.com/**",
"http://localhost:*/**"
],
"token-validity": 3600,
"refresh-token-validity": 86400
}
}

entry.title: User is unable to login to SAP Build Work Zone due to "Subaccount reached its limit" message
entry.content:
Hello,

I'm having an issue where our developer team is unable to login to the SAP build work zone free version. The error they are getting is - " Subaccount reached its limit. Unfortunately, we couldn't log you in because your subaccount has reached the maximum limit of a free plan. A free plan is restricted to 20 named users and 2 admin users per month per subaccount ".

My doubts are as below regarding this: -

1. Can we restrict this admin access on role collections level from security side? Please advise how this can be done

2. Can this error be reset within the day? Cause our developer team needs to check an issue.

 

Regards,

Arpit

SAP Security

entry.title: Security breaches in FTP - SAP Communication
entry.content:
Hi,

I am looking for help on a partner question.  If security breaches are discovered on BTP:

  • how are SAP are resolving them and what happens if a security issue appears, that involves customer actions?
  • how are we communicating them to partners? Is it only via the Monthly Security Patch Day & SAP Security Notes?
  • do we have any document or whitepaper explaining the process?

Thanks

Sheena

entry.title: XSUAA not getting the subject name identifier as ID
entry.content:
Hi,

We're trying to change `req.user.id` used from xsuaa to the User ID of IAS instead of the email.

BTP is connected to IAS with OpenID Connect (so "Default Name ID Format = Unspecified" as with SAML is not available). 

In the IAS logs the sub is changed to the expected value `jwtPayload="{"sub":"`.

However the req.user.id we get in CAP / JWT token remains the email.

Not sure if it's related, but adding a custom atribute in `xs-security.json` and in IAS attributes also isn't reflected in `req.user.attr`. (it only shows the default value configured in `xs-security.json`)

Anyone have some insights on how to get the id ?

entry.title: SCIM3 PATCH API returning 403 Forbidden in SAP Analytics Cloud (GET works, Admin access available)
entry.content:
Hi folks,

I am facing an issue while using the SCIM3 API in SAP Analytics Cloud to update team members using PATCH operation.

API Details

Endpoint:

PATCH  /api/v1/scim3/Groups/<TEAM_UUID>

Authentication:
  • Authorization Bearer Token
Headers Used

  • Authorization: Bearer <access_token>

  • x-csrf-token: <csrf_token>

  • x-sap-sac-custom-auth: true

Payload
"schemas": [
            "urn:ietf:params:scim:api:messages:2.0:PatchOp"
        ],
        "Operations": [
            {
                "op":"add",
                "path":"members",
                "value":[
                    {
                        "value":"d0bbac78-776890-9876hjmd",
                        "type":"User",
                        "display":"newuser",
                        "$ref":"/api/v1/scim3/Users/d0bbac78-776890-9876hjmd"
                    }
                ]
            },
            {
                "op": "replace",
            "path": "urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:extension:sap:2.0:Group:description",
            "value": "patch test"
            }
        ]
Issue

The PATCH request consistently returns:

{
"status": 403,
"error":"Forbidden"
}

Observations

  • GET operations on /api/v1/scim3/Groups are working successfully.
  • Team UUID is retrieved correctly.
  • Team type is userGroup.
  • I have full admin access (BI_Admin role) in SAP Analytics Cloud.
  • OAuth Client is configured with API access with App Integration.
  • CSRF Token is fetched and passed correctly.
  • when CSRF token is removed, the API returns 401 Unauthorized.
  • PATCH always returns 403 Forbidden.
Questions
  1. Are there additional permissions required specifically for SCIM3 PATCH operations ?
  2. Does the OAuth client require explicit SCIM provisioning(write) permissions?
  3. Are there any tenant-level restrictions for SCIM3 write operations ?
Attachments
  • PATCH request (headers + payload)

 

  • Response (403 Forbidden)

Any guidance would be helpful

Thanks!

entry.title: Transactions in identity lifecycle management
entry.content:
Hi

I am new to SAP, and I have a question re this picture in one of the courses:

The best practice in cryptography is to rely on stateless workflows. That is, at the time of authentication an identity provider would issue e.g., a JWT with all authorizations for resources the user is entitled to. The token could then be used in OAuth/ zero-trust environment for transparent access to the SAP cloud on the right-hand side. I would imagine the green arrow "Identity Lifecycle Management" only injects public keys a JWT would be verified against. But because those keys are public, they would usually be pulled from PKI whenever needed, so the push model (injection) is superfluous. I suspect my understanding could be wrong - if someone could please clarify? Is this authentication/authorization workflow a common place among Cloud foundry, ABAP, and Kyma? Maybe you could point me out to a good online course for this?

entry.title: Support Needed: OAuth2 SAML Bearer Destination connection to Successfactor Issue in SAP BTP
entry.content:
Dear Connections,

I’m reaching out regarding an issue we are currently facing with the OAuth2.0 SAML Bearer Assertion destination setup in SAP BTP for establishing a connection with SuccessFactors.

Background

We are working on integrating our CAP-based application deployed on SAP BTP with SuccessFactors using a destination configured with OAuth2SAMLBearerAssertion authentication.

Current Status

  • The destination works successfully with Basic Authentication
  • However, when switching to OAuth2.0 SAML Bearer Assertion, the connection fails

Error Details

  • We are encountering the error:
    “No user token (JWT) has been provided. This is strictly necessary for 'OAuth2SAMLBearerAssertion'.","stacktrace":["Error: No user token (JWT) has been provided. This is strictly necessary for 'OAuth2SAMLBearerAssertion'."”

What We Have Verified

  • Destination is correctly configured in BTP
  • OAuth client is created in SuccessFactors
  • JWT token is available and being passed from CAP (via request headers)
  • User attributes (logonName) are available in the request context
  • CAP application is correctly bound to XSUAA and Destination services

Suspected Areas

  • OAuth configuration mismatch 
  • Certificate/trust configuration between BTP and SuccessFactors
  • SAML assertion generation issues via XSUAA

Request

Since this setup involves multiple components (CAP, XSUAA, Destination service, and SuccessFactors), I wanted to check if anyone have experience with similar OAuth2 SAML Bearer configurations in BTP.

It would be really helpful if you could:

  • Suggest any checks or troubleshooting steps we might be missing

CAPM Code:

const { executeHttpRequest } = require("@sap-cloud-sdk/http-client");
const { getDestination } = require('@sap-cloud-sdk/connectivity');
module.exports = async function (request) {
         let payload = request.data;
        oLogger.info("Workflow Payload: " + JSON.stringify(payload));
        const workflowPath = `odata/v2/upsert?workflowConfirmed=true`;
        oLogger.info("Workflow Path: " + workflowPath);
        payload["__metadata"] = { "uri": "Position" };
const destination = await getDestination({
        destinationName: "sfAdminDestination",
        userJwt
        });
const workflowRes = await executeHttpRequest(
            destination,
            {
                method: "POST",
                url: "odata/v2/upsert?workflowConfirmed=true",
                data: payload,
                headers: {
                    "Content-Type": "application/json",
                    "Accept": "application/json"
                }
            }
        );

}
Thanks in advance for your support.

entry.title: I am SAP security consultant , now I want to learn BTP , how should I prepare ?
entry.content:
Hello all 

I am SAP security consultant, I want to learn BTP, how should I start? Where will I get material and how should I prepare ?

entry.title: Agentic AI in SAP Landscapes: The Next Identity & Authorization Risk Frontier
entry.content:
The adoption of agentic AI across SAP BTP, SAP AI services, and enterprise integrations is introducing a new class of identity risk—one that traditional SAP authorization models were never designed to handle.

At the center of this shift is Agent Identity & Credential Exposure, driven by the rapid expansion of Non-Human Identities (NHIs) across SAP and non-SAP orchestration layers.

The Architectural Mismatch in SAP Environments

Modern SAP AI-driven architectures (e.g., SAP AI Core, automation agents, integration flows) are generating a growing identity surface:

 

  • Technical users in SAP S/4HANA
  • Communication users in SAP BTP
  • OAuth clients and service keys
  • API tokens in SAP Integration Suite
  • Destination service credentials
  • RFC users and background jobs

 

These identities are often:

 

  • Long-lived
  • Over-provisioned
  • Poorly governed across lifecycle

 

Where the mismatch happens

SAP’s traditional authorization model is built around:

 

  • Human users (business roles via PFCG roles)
  • Static authorization objects
  • Clearly defined transactions and scopes

 

However, agentic AI introduces:

 

  • No human-triggered execution context
  • Dynamic, multi-step workflows across systems
  • Runtime decision-making and tool chaining

 

Resulting Gap

Agents frequently:

 

  • Inherit technical user credentials or OAuth tokens
  • Operate with broad composite roles
  • Execute across multiple SAP systems without re-authorization

 

These credentials are then:

 

  • Passed through integration flows
  • Used across APIs and destinations
  • Embedded into orchestration logic or memory layers

 

…without re-validation of authorization scope.

NHI Sprawl in SAP Landscapes

This leads to a new class of SAP-specific identity risk:

 

  • Explosion of technical users across S/4, BTP, and integrations
  • Service keys and OAuth clients without ownership
  • Credentials embedded in: iFlows (Integration Suite) Destinations CI/CD pipelines AI orchestration layers

 

Key Characteristics

 

  • Long-lived RFC and API users
  • Overloaded roles (SAP_ALL-like risk patterns)
  • Lack of traceability between agent → action → business context

 

Attacker Perspective: Exploiting SAP Identity Weakness

Attackers don’t need to compromise SAP core directly.

They target the weakest integration or agent boundary.

Entry Points

 

  • Compromised SAP Integration Suite iFlow
  • Misconfigured BTP destination
  • Exposed service key or OAuth client
  • Prompt injection via AI retrieval (documents, knowledge bases)

 

Once inside:

 

  • A single technical user may have: Cross-module access (FI, MM, HR) Read/write across business data
  • Shared credentials mask identity: All actions appear as one “technical user”

 

Advanced SAP-Specific Attack Paths

 

  • Extracting credentials from: Integration logs CPI traces Application logs in BTP
  • Leveraging over-authorized roles: Triggering unintended transactions (e.g., mass data extraction)
  • Moving laterally across: S/4HANA → BTP → external APIs

 

Illustrative SAP Scenario

An AI agent on SAP BTP is orchestrating a workflow:

 

  • Uses a destination to connect to S/4HANA
  • Authenticates via a technical communication user
  • That user has broad access via composite roles

 

A sub-agent:

 

  • Inherits the same credentials
  • Processes data retrieved from a document store

 

A prompt injection occurs via retrieved content.

Outcome

 

  • Agent executes unintended API calls
  • Extracts sensitive business data (e.g., financial records, vendor data)
  • Data is pulled from CDS views or APIs
  • Activity appears as legitimate system behavior

 

No clear trace to which agent or task triggered the action.

Impact in SAP Context

 

  • Exposure of sensitive ERP data (FI, HR, supply chain)
  • Violations of SoD (Segregation of Duties) principles
  • Compliance breaches (GDPR, SOX)
  • Audit gaps due to shared technical identities
  • Complex forensic investigations across SAP + BTP

 

Mitigation: Re-Architect Identity for SAP AI

1. Per-Agent Identity in SAP

 

  • Assign unique identities per agent: Separate OAuth clients in BTP Distinct communication users in S/4
  • Avoid shared technical users
  • Enable traceability: Agent → Action → System

 

2. Task-Scoped Authorization (Critical for SAP)

 

  • Replace static roles with: Task-based access (least privilege)
  • Avoid: SAP_ALL Broad composite roles

 

Instead:

 

  • Generate dynamic, minimal authorization scopes per API/task
  • Use short-lived tokens for each interaction

 

3. Modernize SAP Authentication Patterns

Move away from static credentials:

 

  • Use OAuth 2.0 Client Credentials with scoped access
  • Implement Workload Identity Federation (BTP)
  • Avoid storing credentials in: Destinations iFlows Code repositories

 

4. Govern Technical Users & NHIs

 

  • Maintain real-time inventory of: Technical users (SU01) OAuth clients Service keys
  • Enforce: Short-lived credentials Automated rotation Immediate revocation

 

5. Strengthen Secret Management

 

  • Use secure vaults (e.g., BTP credential store)
  • Prevent secrets from appearing in: Logs (SLG1, CPI logs) AI prompts or memory
  • Enforce strict rotation policies

 

6. Enforce Authorization Context in SAP

 

  • Map agent actions to: Business roles Authorization objects
  • Ensure: No execution without explicit context
  • Align with Zero Trust principles

 

7. Monitoring & Detection Across SAP + BTP

 

  • Enable: Audit logs (S/4, BTP) Integration monitoring
  • Detect anomalies: Unusual API volume Cross-module access patterns Unauthorized data extraction

 

SAP-Aligned Maturity Model

Tier 1 — Foundational

 

  • Clean up technical users
  • Remove SAP_ALL access
  • Enforce least privilege (PFCG roles)
  • Secure destinations and service keys

 

Tier 2 — Hardening

 

  • Task-scoped OAuth in BTP
  • Continuous discovery of technical identities
  • Monitoring across Integration Suite + S/4

 

Tier 3 — Advanced

 

  • Agent-specific identities with cryptographic trust
  • Full traceability across SAP landscape
  • Elimination of shared credentials
  • Dynamic authorization aligned to runtime context

 

Closing Perspective

SAP landscapes were not designed for autonomous agents operating across:

 

  • Systems
  • APIs
  • Business processes

 

Applying traditional SAP authorization models to agentic AI leads to:

 

  • Over-privileged technical users
  • Invisible identity propagation
  • Undetectable cross-system risks

 

The future of SAP security will depend on:

 

  • Containing NHI sprawl
  • Enforcing task-level authorization in SAP
  • Integrating identity across S/4, BTP, and AI layers

entry.title: IAS App-to-App Navigation Failing: UI5 AppRouter → CAP Node.js via Destination
entry.content:
Hi experts,

I’m trying to implement SAP Cloud Identity Services (IAS / AMS) based authentication between two separately deployed BTP applications (MTAs), but the token exchange from UI5 AppRouter to CAP backend keeps failing.

I’d appreciate guidance on whether my architecture is valid, and what configuration I may have missed.

Landscape

Application A (Consumer)

UI5 frontend with standalone AppRouter

  • Deployed in BTP Cloud Foundry

  • Uses IAS login

  • Calls backend via Destination Service

Application B (Provider)

CAP Node.js service

  • Separate MTA

  • Protected by IAS / AMS

  • Has provided-apis configured

Expected Flow:

User
→ UI5 AppRouter
→ Destination (IASDependencyName)
→ AppRouter exchanges token
→ CAP backend receives exchanged token
→ CAP authorizes user

Current Destination Setup

Name: CAP_AMS_BACKEND
Type: HTTP
Authentication: NoAuthentication
URL: https://<cap-service-url>

Additional Properties:
HTML5.IASDependencyName = cap-ams //setup in IAS

I have also tried with destination type = oAuth2UserTokenExchange but got the same 401 error. Error screenshot below. Looks like the destination service client Id is the caller to IAS for token exchange but IAS is rejecting the audience.

UI5 App xs-app.json

{
  "welcomeFile": "/index.html",
  "authenticationMethod": "route",
  "routes": [
    {
      "source": "^/odata/(.*)$",
      "target": "/odata/$1",
      "destination": "CAP_AMS_BACKEND",
      "authenticationType": "ias"
    },
    {
      "source": "^(.*)$",
      "localDir": "resources",
      "authenticationType": "ias"
    }
  ]
}

 CAP App ias instance

UI5 App ias instance

 

Dependency set on Consumer --> Provider App in IAS

All the grant types are selected in the provider and the consumer app for testing

 
What I Want to Confirm:
  1. Is this architecture officially supported? Standalone UI5 AppRouter (MTA A) calling separate CAP Node.js app (MTA B) via destination using IAS App-to-App navigation?
  2. Should this work using only:
    Authentication = NoAuthentication / oAuth2UserTokenExchange
    HTML5.IASDependencyName = <dependencyName>
  3. Should both apps share the same IAS instance, or is separate IAS instance + dependency the correct model?
  4. Has anyone implemented this successfully with CAP Node.js?

Thanks in advance,

Deepak

entry.title: Unable to View Subaccount in BTP Cockpit After CIS Trust Setup (403 – Global Account Undefined)
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I am facing an issue accessing a BTP subaccount when logging in via SAP Cloud Identity Services (CIS), even though trust and role assignments are in place.

Setup details:

  • Trust configured between CIS tenant and SAP BTP Global Account (for platform users)
  • Login performed using the BTP cockpit URL with https://amer.cockpit.btp.cloud.sap/cockpit/?idp=<CIAS_TENANT>
  • Platform user:
    • Successfully authenticates via CIS
    • Added to the target subaccount
    • Assigned Subaccount Viewer role collection

Issue observed:

  • After login, the BTP cockpit shows:
    • “Global Account: undefined – Account Explorer”
    • 403 error: “Could not load global account”
  • No global account, directories, or subaccounts are visible

Based on the role assignment, at least read-only access to the subaccount was expected.

You can refer below image

Questions:

  1. Are there any mandatory Global Account–level role collections (e.g. Global Account Viewer / Directory Viewer) required for CIS-authenticated platform users to see subaccounts?
  2. Is there any additional setup or user assignment step required beyond:
    • CIS trust configuration
    • Subaccount role collection assignment?

Any guidance or pointers on missing prerequisites or common pitfalls for CIS + BTP cockpit access would be helpful.

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: 403 ForbiddenThe request has been blocked by UCON
entry.content:
Hi Team,

I am trying the RAP application in ABAP BTP cockpit and on clicking the Service URL I get to see " 403 Forbidde The request has been blocked by UCON." message on the screen.

And If I click on preview I see the screen but no fields appear on the FIORI app to select the fields.


I have Created a CDS View without UI annotations, and on top of that I have created a Service Definition and Service Binding on the same.

Any Idea, what needs to be done to get the metadata URL?
https://afb8637d-43ab-41e3-8743-e5ea9e7a5e6f.abap-web.us10.hana.ondemand.com/sap/opu/odata/sap/ZKSR_SB_CONNECTION/$metadata

Thanks!

entry.title: S4 Post Upgrade - Profile Generator (SU25) Options
entry.content:
Hi All ,

While performing the security upgrade (SU25) as part of the S/4HANA system migration from S/4HANA 2022 SP03 to S/4HANA 2025 SP01, we observed that several new options were introduced in SU25. The impact of these changes is not clearly explained in the SAP documentation available to us. As a result, many of our roles are being affected—new authorization objects are being introduced, some existing ones are being removed automatically, and custom SU24 updates appear to be overwritten.

Before proceeding further, we would appreciate guidance on the impact of each selection under SU25 steps 2a, 2b, 2c, 2d, etc. If any detailed documentation or guidance is available, please share it.

As a precaution, we are also considering taking a User Master export post-upgrade, but before executing SU25, along with a backup of the SU24 customizing tables (USOBT_C and USOBX_C). We would like confirmation on whether these backups can be used to restore the system to its pre-SU25 state if required.

entry.title: accessing SAP BTP Trial fail due to verifying phone number
entry.content:
hi Senior,

i am facing issue when accessing SAP BTP Trial get fail due to verifying phone number.

error message "We did not send you a code because you are not allowed to request phone calls. Please contact your system administrator."

anyone getting the same issue ?.

how to solve it ?

entry.title: SAP BTP CF CAPI: replacement for password grant (OAuth 2.1 / RFC 9700)
entry.content:
I'm building an internal governance integration on SAP BTP (euXX) that **orchestrates org and space membership** in our Cloud Foundry subaccounts. The integration runs as SAP Cloud Integration (CPI) iFlows: incoming trigger from IAM, the iFlow validates and calls the Cloud Foundry v3 API to assign or revoke roles.

**Scope of this question**: only managing **organization and space members** — i.e. `POST /v3/roles`, `DELETE /v3/roles/{guid}`, and the related `GET /v3/roles`, `/v3/users`, `/v3/organizations`, `/v3/spaces` calls needed to resolve names to GUIDs.  

Calling `/v3/*` requires a Bearer token issued by the **platform CF UAA** (`https://login.cf.eu10.hana.ondemand.com/oauth/token`), not by a tenant XSUAA — confirmed multiple times on this Community (e.g. https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/how-to-access-cloud-foundry-controller-api/qaq-p/12144361).

Constraints I have to live with:

  • `grant_type=password` (which I'm using now to connect to CF) is out (deprecated by OAuth 2.1 / RFC 9700)
  • It seems I don't have direct `uaac` admin access to the platform UAA https://docs.cloudfoundry.org/api/uaa/version/78.13.0/index.html , so I can't just `uaac client add my-cpi-client --scope cloud_controller.write`.  The user I use has the global admin and orgManager of the subaccount.

Is there a way to give access to CF v3 APIs via client credent?

I also tried using the CF CLI this way on the XSUAA client, but it returns that my user is unauthorized.

cf8 set-org-role "clientID-XSUAA" ORGNAME "OrgManager" --client

References already consulted:

  • CF v3 API reference — https://v3-apidocs.cloudfoundry.org/
  • CF UAA API reference — https://docs.cloudfoundry.org/api/uaa/
  • BTP docs — *About Roles in the Cloud Foundry Environment*
  • RFC 9700 (OAuth 2.0 Security BCP)  https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/draft-ietf-oauth-v2-1 
  • https://oauth.net/2/grant-types/password/#:~:text=The%20Password%20grant%20type%20is,be%20used%20at%20all%20anymore

Thank you in advance,

Giulio

entry.title: Guidance Needed for SAP Learning Journey and SAP Security Career Path
entry.content:
Dear SAP Learning and Community Team,

I hope you are doing well.

I am currently studying for a Bachelor’s degree in Cybersecurity and IT at Rutgers University, and I recently received access to SAP Learning resources and certifications through my university opportunity.

I am very interested in becoming SAP certified and building a future career related to SAP, cybersecurity, enterprise systems, and emerging technologies. I am also actively learning and exploring AI, and I am very interested in understanding how AI, cybersecurity, and SAP technologies may connect in future enterprise environments.

However, I am currently a complete beginner in SAP and do not yet have prior knowledge of ERP systems, SAP modules, accounting concepts, FI/CO, MM, business processes, T-codes, or SAP terminology.

As I started exploring the SAP Learning portal, I found there are many learning journeys, modules, and specialization paths, which made it difficult for me to understand where I should begin and what sequence I should follow.

Since my academic background is in Cybersecurity and IT, I would sincerely appreciate guidance on the best SAP learning roadmap that aligns with my studies and long-term career goals. I would especially value recommendations related to SAP Security, GRC, governance, access management, enterprise security, AI-related SAP technologies, or other cybersecurity-relevant SAP areas.

I would be very grateful if your team could kindly guide me regarding:

1. Which beginner learning journeys I should start with from scratch
2. How I should learn ERP concepts, SAP basics, navigation, and business processes step by step
3. Whether I should first learn FI/CO, MM, T-codes, Internal Orders, Profit Centers, and SAP fundamentals before moving into SAP Security
4. The recommended sequence of courses and learning journeys for someone with a Cybersecurity and IT background
5. Which SAP certifications would be most relevant and valuable for my future career goals
6. Which learning journey could eventually lead me toward SAP Security or SAP GRC certification paths
7. Whether there are SAP learning paths that combine AI, enterprise systems, and cybersecurity concepts

My goal is to follow a proper structured roadmap instead of randomly selecting courses without understanding the correct direction.

I would sincerely appreciate your guidance and recommendations.

Thank you very much for your time and support.

Kind regards,
Inaya

entry.title: How to find version of sap ETD( SAP enterprise threat detection) in sap netweaver 758 SP01
entry.content:
Hi All,

We are working on POC for SAP EDT cloud version with direct integration from SAP BTP to our RISE sap system. , While we try to configure SAP ETD in SAP system we are stuck when we sending data for our client (giving error CX_SETD_HTTP_403). I opened a ticket with SAP support but no luck till now.

I have few question to experts if anyone can help here.

1. How to find version of sap ETD( SAP enterprise threat detection) in sap NetWeaver 758 SP01 as it integrated with NW 758

2. On SAP system ETD launchpad is (version 0.3). Do we need to upgrade it on NetWeaver 758 SP01.

3. Any suggestion to resolve above error?

entry.title: How to use APIs to retrieve BTP Audit Logs and Roles at Global Account level?
entry.content:
We are trying to access the audit logs and users and roles from Business hub APIs.

We need to get data for Global account as Audit log and also users, roles, and role collection data through API for a BTP Global account.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: SAC and BTP subaccount Provisioning
entry.content:
Need clarity on below items 

 

1. Can we provision to SAC and BTP subaccount directly via IPS without IAG
2. If yes , can we provision to SAC and BTP subaccount directly via IPS without IAG if request triggers via GRC

entry.title: SAP IAS connection to SAP BTP via OIDC is not working
entry.content:
I have established the connectivity between IAS and BTP using OIDC via the "establish trust" button in trust configuration. All the necessary configuration is also done. ROle collection is also provided under trust configuration.

However, when I am assigning the user in IAS, it says missing authorization while logging to BTP. 

It is not creating the use in the subaccount as well. 

Any suggestions please? 

I can go for SAML but Joule enablement needs only OIDC. Hence I am stuck.
